sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: 2046 (2004) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Wong Kar-Wai's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 2046 clicked for you, Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after 2046?
faq.answer: Try Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind, Her, Upside Down—each shares DNA with 2046 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 2046?
faq.answer: 2046 (2004) is best described as Drama, Science Fiction, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 2046 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "In the Mood for Love Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
